title: annona reticulata var. primigenia
intro: Annona reticulata var. primigenia, also known as the wild custard apple, is a variant of the custard apple tree native to tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Annona reticulata var. primigenia include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves, bark, and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions.
